_schema: 6-beat structure per CLAUDE.md law 5. Prose drafted from placeholders; land_read text preserved verbatim (Director canon).
_status: ai_draft_needs_director_curation (2026-07-06) — all prose except land_read is AI draft

intro_cold.speaker: sandy
variants: Weigh in or move the truck.

intro.speaker: sandy
intro.text:
Line's been steady.

You hauling or asking?
variants:
Scale's running true.

Checked it myself this morning, same as every morning.

If you're here to argue moisture numbers, the machine wins.

It always wins.

Morning.

Dock's clear for about twenty minutes. That's a fact, not an invitation.

text_rules.text:
You're the one helped me clear that Tuesday backup.

Go ahead of the grain trucks — they're all waiting on moisture tests anyway.

options.text: "Give me a week with your intake times. I can shorten this line."
options.label: [IT Nephew]

options.text: "That field's telling on itself before the leaves do."
options.label: [Read the Land]

intro_trusted.speaker: sandy
intro_trusted.text:
Slow morning.

First one this month, so I'm suspicious of it.

Beans been coming in wetter than folks want to admit, and the dryer's been running since Friday.

How's your ground holding up?
variants:
Pull around, you know where.

Dryer's been going since Friday — beans coming in wet and nobody admitting it.

Hollis weighed in Tuesday, asked after you.

Sort of. In his way.

options.text: "Holding. Ask me again after the next rain."

timing_success.speaker: sandy
timing_success.text:
Ran your schedule for a week.

Line moved.

You unload first from now on — don't make a speech about it.

timing_failure.speaker: sandy
timing_failure.text:
Your model backed my Tuesday line up two hours.

Every farmer sitting in it knows whose idea that was.

land_read.speaker: sandy
land_read.text:
Most folks wait till damage shows.

You still know how to listen.

chat.speaker: sandy
chat.text:
Corn's moving through here faster than it did in June.

Make of that what you want.
variants:
Three guys hauled beans early this week.

Either they know something or they need cash.

Usually it's cash.

Dryer's been running since Friday.

Wet grain, soft prices. That's not advice, it's arithmetic.

Nobody's hauling.

When nobody's hauling, somebody's waiting on a number.

Same trucks, same order, all week.

County's in a holding pattern.
